com.mentor.chs.plugin.constraint

Interface IXCavityMappingConstraint

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXConstraint, IXPlugin

    public interface IXCavityMappingConstraint
extends IXConstraint
    Custom Constraint - used to control the routing of wires of a given signal.

    This constraint is provided to help provide control over which cavity (or cavities) a particular signal (or set of signals) will map to.


    If this constraint's match(..) method returns true then the cavity mapping result (IXCavityMappingResult parameter) will be taken into consideration during the route. If the result specifies a DO parameter then this constraint will specify the cavity (or cavities) that are to be preferred when mapping the given signals to inline cavities.

    If, however, the result specifies a DON'T value for the result then the cavities contained within the result are restrictions rather than preferences.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods Default Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      boolean match(IXSignal signal, IXConnector connector, IXCavityMappingResult result)
      Determine if constraint applies given these objects.
      default boolean match(IXSignal signal, IXRingTerminal ringTerminal, IXCavityMappingResult result)
      Determine if constraint applies given these objects.

      • Methods inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.plugin.IXPlugin

        getDescription, getName, getVersion

    • Method Detail

      • match

        boolean match(IXSignal signal,
              IXConnector connector,
              IXCavityMappingResult result)
        Determine if constraint applies given these objects. If so, return a result to indicate the proper action.
        Parameters:
        signal - - To be mapped to cavity
        connector - - The connector that has the cavities being considered
        result - - Return a result that describes the cavities to either map or explicitly not map to.
        Returns:
        boolean - Does the constraint apply to this specific set of objects?

      • match

        default boolean match(IXSignal signal,
                      IXRingTerminal ringTerminal,
                      IXCavityMappingResult result)
        Determine if constraint applies given these objects. If so, return a result to indicate the proper action.
        Parameters:
        signal - - To be mapped to cavity
        ringTerminal - - The ringTerminal that has the cavity being considered
        result - - Return a result that describes the cavity to either map or explicitly not map to.
        Returns:
        boolean - Does the constraint apply to this specific set of objects ? The defaut implementation returns false
